The plant is classified as a perennial, producing long, linear leaves and small white or purple flowers. Within the family Asparagaceae, Earleocassia roemeriana is often placed in the subfamily Brodiaeoideae. This classification is based on the plant's growth habit, leaf structure, and flower characteristics.
